rfi.subject: Conflict between architectural reflected ceiling plan and mechanical drawings — Data Hall A north corridor
rfi.body: Architectural sheet A4.12 RCP shows a continuous T-bar grid at elevation 14'-6" AFF along the north corridor of Data Hall A (gridlines A.1–A.7 / 12–18). Mechanical sheet M4.04 shows a 36" round chilled-water main routed at 14'-2" AFF in the same corridor with a 4" insulation jacket, putting the bottom of insulation at 13'-10" AFF. There is no clearance for ceiling tile removal for filter access. Please confirm: (a) is the chilled-water main allowed to drop the corridor ceiling height to 13'-6" AFF, (b) should we route the main above the architectural ceiling at 15'-0", which conflicts with the structural 18" beam pocket on S3.04, or (c) issue a design change. The CMU walls in this area are scheduled to top out next Tuesday, so we are blocked on coordination beyond Friday this week.

spec_index.excerpt: §3.4.A Chilled-water mains in occupied corridors shall maintain 9'-0" minimum AFF clearance to bottom of insulation.
§2.1.B Acoustical T-bar grid: 14'-6" AFF in all data hall corridors except as noted on the RCP.

drawings.excerpt: RCP — Data Hall A north corridor — T-bar @ 14'-6" AFF
36" CHWS routed N corridor, BOP 14'-2" AFF, 4" insulation
